title: NZ School Holidays Travel Tips — How to Beat the Crowds and Save Money
meta_desc: Travel NZ during school holidays without breaking the bank. Get tips on beating crowds and saving money
intro: Travelling during NZ school holidays can be expensive, with prices for accommodation and activities increasing by up to 50% during peak season. However, with some planning and insider knowledge, you can still have a great family holiday without breaking the bank. For example, a recent trip to Rotorua during the school holidays cost our family $800 for a 3-night stay in a motel, compared to $1200 during the same period the previous year, simply by booking earlier and opting for a self-contained unit

sections.heading: Understanding Peak and Off-Peak Pricing
sections.body: In New Zealand, peak season typically falls during the school holidays, which are usually in April, July, and October. During this time, prices for accommodation, activities, and tourist attractions tend to increase. However, if you can travel during the off-peak season, you can save a significant amount of money. For instance, a 2-hour guided tour of the Hobbiton Movie Set in Matamata costs $89 per adult and $44 per child during peak season, but $79 per adult and $39 per child during off-peak season.

sections.heading: Booking Lead Times
list: Book your accommodation at least 6 months in advance to secure the best rates
Consider booking a self-contained unit or apartment, which can be more cost-effective than a hotel room
Look for packages that include activities and meals, which can sometimes be cheaper than booking individual components separately

sections.heading: Driving Times and Distances
sections.body: When planning your trip, it's essential to consider the driving times and distances between destinations. For example, the drive from Auckland to Rotorua takes approximately 3 hours, while the drive from Wellington to Queenstown takes around 10 hours. Be sure to factor in rest stops and meal breaks to avoid fatigue and keep your family safe on the road.

sections.heading: Affordable Activities
list: Visit the Auckland Museum, which offers free admission for children under 5 and $25 for adults
Take a hike in the beautiful Waitakere Ranges, which offers free admission and stunning views
Explore the Wellington Zoo, which costs $27 per adult and $16 per child

cta.text: Use our NZ Family Travel Budget Calculator to plan your trip
Discover more family-friendly destinations in New Zealand
